title: Computing Matrix Inverse using CHT
description: Algorithm to find inverse of a non-singular matrix using Cayley-Hamilton Theorem
code:
FUNCTION InverseCHT(A)
 n ← SIZE(A)
 // Compute characteristic polynomial coefficients
 // p(λ) = λ^n + c_{n-1}λ^{n-1} + ... + c_1λ + c_0
 
 I ← IDENTITY_MATRIX(n)
 B[0] ← I
 c[0] ← -TRACE(A)
 B[1] ← A * B[0] + c[0] * I
 
 FOR k FROM 2 TO n DO
 c[k-1] ← -(1/k) * TRACE(A * B[k-1])
 B[k] ← A * B[k-1] + c[k-1] * I
 END FOR
 
 // c[0] = c_0 = (-1)^n * det(A)
 IF c[0] == 0 THEN
 RETURN "Matrix is singular, no inverse"
 END IF
 
 // Compute A^(-1) = -(A^(n-1) + c_{n-1}A^(n-2) + ... + c_1*I) / c_0
 inv ← -(B[n-1] + c[n-2]*B[n-2] + ... + c[1]*I) / c[0]
 
 RETURN inv
END FUNCTION
explanation: Uses Faddeev-LeVerrier algorithm to compute coefficients, then applies the formula for inverse using characteristic polynomial. Time complexity: O(n⁴) for n×n matrix.

title: Computing Matrix Powers using CHT
description: Efficiently compute A^k for large k using power reduction
explanation: Uses the characteristic polynomial to reduce matrix powers efficiently. For k > n-1, reduces to linear combination of lower powers using CHT identity.

title: Faddeev-LeVerrier Algorithm
description: Systematic computation of characteristic polynomial coefficients
explanation: Computes coefficients recursively using matrix traces. Each iteration builds intermediate matrices B_k. Final polynomial: p(λ) = λ^n + c_{n-1}λ^{n-1} + ... + c_0.
